nodes.title: Note- READ ME
widgets_values:
-------------------------------
 RULES NEEDED FOR THIS TO WORK
-------------------------------

This works best for AI generated audio.

This node will look for gaps greater than > silence_threshold (for example 1 second)

So any time there is gap of more than 1 second it causes that a new speaker to be assigned to the next segment.

In TTS workflows you can use something like

[pause:1.0] 

to create a 1 second pause between speakers.


If you want to use real audio, you will have to edit the audio with audacity and make sure you insert some silence between each speaker based on the "silence_threshold"

Click on the "Detect Speakers" button to scan the audio file and find the speaker segments based on the silence threshold.

You can always manually set the values yourself if you dont want to modify audio files.

**************LTX 2.3 VERSION*************

https://huggingface.co/Lightricks/LTX-2.3/tree/main  (upscaler)
https://huggingface.co/QuantStack/LTX-2.3-GGUF/tree/main/LTX-2.3-distilled (main gguf)
https://huggingface.co/Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy/tree/main (vae, text projector)

MODELS NEEDED

./models/text_encoders
gemma-3-12b-it-Q2_K.gguf                   (this is only 4GB for low vram)
ltx-2.3_text_projection_bf16.safetensors

./models/unet  (DISTILLED version, distilled only needs 8 steps)
LTX-2.3-distilled-Q5_K_S.gguf              (Distilled version provides benefits for low vram)

./models/vae (2.3)
LTX23_audio_vae_bf16.safetensors
LTX23_video_vae_bf16.safetensors
taeltx_2.safetensors                       (Need FOR PREVIEWs)

./models/latent_upscale_models (2.3)
ltx-2.3-spatial-upscaler-x2-1.0.safetensors

If you can't find my custom nodes in Manager (Handy Nodes), then use this

git clone https://github.com/trashkollector/TKNodes
